summary: High‑energy 1991 show captured on solid front‑of‑house matrix recording; the band is in top form with a smoking "Same Thing" and strong second set.
review: Fans rave about this December 28‑91 concert as one of the best of the ’90s, highlighting Jerry’s soaring guitar, Phil’s romping bass, Brent’s rollicking keys and a standout performance of The Same Thing that had the crowd in awe.  The recording is an audience (FOB) matrix source – clear enough to enjoy the full band dynamics with only occasional mild drop‑outs noted by a few reviewers.

ai_rating.rationale: All seven reviews are strongly positive about the performance, and most comment that the front‑of‑house recording is good to excellent; weighted combination yields a rating above 4.5.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Front‑of‑house matrix capture using B&K 4011 cardioids and a Neumann BS‑48i; reviewers call it "very quality FOB sound" and praise the mix, though one notes the sound is about 3.5–4/5 with occasional mild drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The show opens with a seamless jam that melts into Jack Straw, builds through a powerful second‑set climax centered on The Same Thing, and closes with high‑energy encores like One More Saturday Night and U.S. Blues, delivering an exciting, well‑paced arc.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as "screaming and swooping", delivering outrageous double‑timed runs and a soaring vocal on Uncle John's Band; overall his playing is highlighted as the night’s high point.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for romping all night long, especially during the Minglewood jam, showing strong bass presence throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned slipping a lyric early but quickly finding his voice; credited as the "glue" holding the set together and delivering solid rhythm work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland (Vinny) noted for rollicking, perfect keyboard textures that added depth to the jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Both Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann are said to have "banged and drove it" especially on Uncle John's Band, providing a tight rhythmic foundation.
